title: capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. bursa-pastoris
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. bursa-pastoris, also known as shepherd's purse, is a plant in the mustard family that is native to Europe and Asia but has naturalized in many other parts of the world. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat menstrual cramps, bleeding, and inflammation, due to its high content of isothiocyanates and glucosinolates. The health benefits of shepherd's purse include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestion, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of shepherd's purse often include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes.
